title: Chickpea Desi Variety ICCL 80074 (ICCC 37).Plant Material Description no.26
abstract: Derived from the 3-way cross P481 X (JG62 X P1630), the Cicer arietinum variety ICCL80074 was released in 1989 under the name Kranthi for general cultivation in Andhra Pradesh. It is semispreading with an upright growth habit and matures in about 100 days. In trials at several sites in Andhra Pradesh in 1982-83, ICCL80074 was ranked highest with an average yield of 2.38 t/ha, compared with 1.86 t/ha for Annigeri. In multilocational trials during 1982-86, mean yield of ICCL80074 was 1.5 t/ha vs. 1.27 t/ha for Annigeri. ICCL80074 gave an average yield of 1.17 t/ha in farmers' fields in Andhra Pradesh in 1988-89, compared with 0.76 t/ha for the local variety Jyothi. Seeds of ICCL80074 are light brown and attractive and 100-seed weight is 18.6 g. Seeds contain 20.6% protein, 6.0% sugar and 9.6% iron and protein digestibility is 64.7%. ICCL80074 is resistant to race 1 of Fusarium oxysporum and moderately tolerant of root rots; Helicoverpa pod damage is slightly reduced when compared with other commonly grown cultivars.
